text: Methyl 7-isopropyl-3-(2-thienylethynyl)azulene-1-carboxylate (9): The procedure used for the preparation of 5 was adopted here. Reaction of 4 (505 mg, 2.00 mmol) with 2-iodothiophene (630 mg, 3.00 mmol) in triethylamine (20 mL) and THF (20 mL) in the presence of Cul (38 mg, tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(o) 0.10 mmol) at 50°C for 3 h followed by column chromatography on silica gel with toluene afforded 9 (623 mg, 93%). Green crystals; m.p. 63.065.0°C (hexane); IR (KBr disk): max = 3107 (m), 2959 (m), 2193 (w), 1687 (s), 1500 (m), 1450 (s), 1415 (s), 1381 (m), 1378 (m), 1236 (s), 1213 (s), 1190 (m), 1167 (m), 1130 (m), 1049 (m), 777 (m), 713 cm ' (m); UV/ Vis (CH,Ch): Amax (loge) =248 (4.38), 278 (4.40), 314 (4.53), 350 sh (4.16), 400 (4.04), 420 sh (3.88), 572 nm (2.81); 'H NMR (500 MHz, CDCI): 8=9.72 (s, 1H, Hg), 8.60 (d, 1H, J=9.5 Hz, H4), 8.44 (s, 1H, H,), 7.78 (d, 1H, J =10.5 Hz, H6), 7.49 (dd, 1H, J =9.5, 10.5 Hz, Hs), 7.31 (dd, 1H, J=4.0, 1.0 Hz, Th-H3), 7.28 (dd, 1H, J=4.0, 1.0 Hz, Th-Hs), 7.02 (dd, 1H, J=4.0, 1.0 Hz, Th-H4), 3.95 (s, 3H, CO,Me), 3.22 (sept, 1H, J=7.0 Hz, iPr), 1.42 ppm (d, 6H, J=7.0 Hz, iPr); 13C NMR (100 MHz, CDCl:): 0 = 165.3, 150.6, 145.2, 144.7, 142.6, 141.2, 139.4, 138.3, 136.2, 131.3, 127.5, 127.1, 126.7, 124.0, 115.1, 108.6, 88.5, 86.5, 51.1, 39.2, 24.5 ppm; HRMS (ESI) calcd for [CH,sO,S+Na]*: 357.0925; found: 357.0920; elemental analysis calcd (%) for CH,sO,S: C 75.42, H

1,4-Bis| (5-isopropyl-3-methoxycarbonyl-1-azulenylethynyl|benzene (6): The procedure used for the preparation of 5 was adopted here. Reaction of 2 (779 mg, 2.20 mmol) with 1,4-diethynylbenzene (126 mg, 1.00 mmol) in triethylamine (10 mL) and THF (10 mL) in the presence of Cul tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(o) (116 mg, 0.10 mmol) at 50°C for 3 h followed by column chromatography on silica gel with CH,Ch afforded 6 (527 mg, 91%). Black crystals; m.p. 238.0-240.0°C (CH,Ch); IR (KBr disk): max = 2961 (m), 2199 (m), 1695 (s), 1491 (m), 1444 (s), 1425 (m), 1408 (m), 1371 (m), 1248 (m), 1209 (s), 1197 (s), 1169 (s), 1072 (m), 1049 (m), 873 (m), 839 (m), 775 cm ' (m); UV/Vis (CH,C12): Amax (loge) =244 (4.71), 276 (4.45), 306 (4.62), 312 (4.76), 356 (4.72), 410 (4.64), 432 sh (4.60), 570 (3.23), 614 sh (3.12), 694 nm sh (2.45); 'H NMR (500 MHz, CDC13): 8=9.72 (s, 2H, Hg), 8.67 (d, 2H, J=9.6 Hz, Ha), 8.48 (s, 2H, H2), 7.82 (d, 2H, J =10.0 Hz, H6), 7.60 (s, 4H, Bz-H23,5,6), 7.53 (dd, 2H, J=9.6, 10.0 Hz, Hs), 3.97 (s, 6H, CO,Me), 3.24 (sept, 2H, J=7.0 Hz, iPr), 1.43 ppm (d, 12H, J=7.0 Hz, iPr); 13C NMR (100 MHz, CDCl;): 8 = 165.4, 150.7, 144.8, 142.8, 141.4, 139.4, 138.4, 136.2, 131.3, 127.6, 123.2, 115.2, 108.9, 93.7, 86.8, 51.2, 39.3, 24.6 ppm; HRMS (ESI) calcd for [CH3404+Na]*: 601.2355; found: 601.2349; elemental analysis calcd (%) for C40H3404⅓H,0: C 82.17, H

(10): The procedure used for the preparation of 5 was adopted here. Rewith 2,5-iodothiophene (504 mg, 1.50 mmol) in triethylamine (20 mL) and THF (20 mL) in the presence of Cul (38 mg, 0.20 mmol) and tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(o) (116 mg, 0.10 mmol) at 50°C for 12 h followed by column chromatography on silica gel with toluene afforded 9 (85 mg, 9%) and 10 (633 mg 72%). Green crystals; m.p. 178.0-179.5°C (AcOEt); IR (KBr disk): Imax = 2957 (m), 2189 (m), 1695 (s), 1498 (m), 1444 (s), 1419 (m), 1371 (m), 1240 (m), 1209 (s), 1167 (m), 804 (m), 775 cm-' (m); UV/Vis (CH, C1): max (loge) =244 (4.75), 282 sh (4.70), 294 (4.72), 310 sh (4.69), 382 sh (4.59), 412 (4.60), 570 nm (3.27); 'H NMR (500 MHz, CDCl): б = 9.75 (s, 2H, Hg), 8.63 (d, 2H, J=10.5 Hz, H4), 8.47 (s, 2H, H2), 7.84 (d 2H, J=10.5 Hz, H6), 7.55 (dd, 2H, J = 10.5, 10.5 Hz, Hs), 7.22 (s, 2H, ThH34), 3.97 (s, 6H, CO,Me), 3.25 (sept, 2H, J=6.5 Hz, iPr), 1.44 ppm (d 12H, J =6.5 Hz, iPr); 1C NMR (100 MHz, CDCl): 8=165.3, 150.8. 144.7, 142.6, 141.3, 139.4, 138.3, 136.2, 131.2, 127.7, 124.8, 115.2, 108.3 89.8, 86.5, 51.1, 39.2, 24.5 ppm; HRMS (ESI) calcd for [C38H32O,S+Na]*: 607.1919; found: 607.1914; elemental analysis calcd (%) for C38H32O4S: C 78.06, H 5.52; found: C 77.90, H 5.49.

(7): The procedure used for the preparation of 5 was adopted here. Reaction of 2 (1.27 g, 3.60 mmol) with 1,3,5-triethynylbenzene (150 mg, 1.00 mmol) in triethylamine (10 mL) and THF (10 mL) in the presence of Cul (38 mg, 0.20 mmol) and tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(o) (116 mg, 0.10 mmol) at 50°C for 2 h followed by column chromatography on silica gel with toluene afforded 7 (700 mg, 84%). Green crystals; m.p. 200.0-205.0°C (AcOEt); IR (KBr disk): max = 2957 (m), 2197 (m), 1693 (s), 1578 (m), 1508 (m), 1148 (s), 1414 (s), 1371 (m), 1215 (s), 1165 cm-1 (m); UV/Vis (CH,Cl,): 2max (loge) = 248 (4.96), 274 sh (4.85), 312 (5.09), 342 (4.92), 400 (4.71), 416 (4.66), 568 (3.37), 608 sh (3.28), 684 nm sh (2.68); 'H NMR (500 MHz, CDCl:): d =9.76 (dd, 3H, Hg), 8.71 (d, 3H, J=9.5 Hz, H4), 8.50 (s, 3H, H2), 7.84 (d, 3H, J=10.5 Hz, H6), 7.79 (s, 3H, Bz-H246), 7.57 (dd, 3H, J=9.5, 10.5 Hz, Hs), 3.98 (s, 9H, CO,Me), 3.26 (sept, 3H, J=7.0 Hz, iPr), 1.44 ppm (d, 18H, J =7.0 Hz, iPr); 13C NMR (100 MHz, CDCl;): 8 = 165.4, 150.8, 145.0, 142.8, 141.4, 139.4, 138.4, 136.3, 133.0, 127.7, 124.7, 115.2, 108.6, 92.5, 86.0, 51.2, 39.3, 24.6 ppm; HRMS (ESI) calcd for [CHsO%+Na]*: 851.3349; found: 851.3343; elemental analysis calcd (%) for C57H4806: C 82.58, H 5.84; found: C 82.46, H 6.04.

Bis(5-isopropyl-3-methoxycarbonyl-1-azulenyl)acetylene (11): The procedure used for the preparation of 5 was adopted here. The reaction of 2 (390 mg, 1.10 mmol) with 4 (252 mg, 1.00 mmol) in triethylamine (10 mL) and THF (10 mL) in the presence of Cul (38 mg, 0.10 mmol) and tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(o) (116 mg, 0.10 mmol) at 50°C for 2 h followed by column chromatography on silica gel with AcOEt/hexane (1:4) afforded 11 (449 mg, 94%). Green crystals; m.p. 178.0-179.0°C (AcOEt); IR (KBr disk): max =2951 (m), 1693 (s), 1454 (m), 1441 (m), 1412 (m), 1373 (m), 1207 (s), 1201 (s), 1172 (m), 1151 (m), 1116 (m), 1074 (m), 871 cm ' (m); UV/Vis (CH,Ch): Amax (loge) =246 (4.68), 288 (4.84), 316 (4.74), 416 (4.33), 452 sh (4.25), 588 nm (3.19); 'H NMR (500 MHz, CDCl:): 8=9.74 (s, 2H, Hg), 8.74 (d, 2H, J =9.5 Hz, H4), 8.54 (s, 2H, H2), 7.81 (d, 2H, J =10.5 Hz, H6), 7.52 (dd, 2H, J =9.5, 10.5 Hz, Hs), 3.98 (s, 6H, CO,Me), 3.24 (sept, 2H, J =7.0 Hz, iPr), 1.44 ppm (d, 12H, J=7.0 Hz, iPr); 1C NMR (100 MHz, CDCl;): 8=165.5, 150.4, 144.5, 142.6, 141.2, 139.2, 138.2, 136.3, 127.3, 115.1, 109.9, 88.7, 51.2, 39.2. 24.6 ppm; HRMS (ESI) calcd for [CH3o04+Na]*: 501.2042; found: 501.2036; elemental analysis calcd (%) for C32H3004: C 80.31, Н 6.32;

zene (8): The procedure used for the preparation of 5 was adopted here. Reaction of 4 (887 mg, 3.50 mmol) with 1,2,4,5-tetraiodobenzene (407 mg, 0.70 mmol) in triethylamine (15 mL) and THF (15 mL) in the presence of Cul (114 mg, 0.60 mmol) and tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(o) (324 mg, 0.30 mmol) at 50°C for 19 h followed by column chromatography on silica gel with CH,Cl afforded 8 (582 mg, 77%). Brown crystals; m.p. > 300°C (AcOEt); IR (KBr disk): Ymax = 2955 (m), 2187 (m), 1693 (s), 1682 (s), 1481 (m), 1448 (s), 1421 (m), 1373 (m), 1223 (s), 1196 (m), 1169 (m), 1103 cm ' (m); UV/Vis (CH,Ch): Amax (logs) = 244 (4.91), 302 (4.95), 354 (4.74), 410 (4.79), 462 (4.49), 568 sh (3.49), 630 sh (3.28), 698 nm sh (2.70); 'H NMR (500 MHz, CDCl,): 8=9.73 (s, 4H, Hg), 8.70 (d, 4H, J=9.5 Hz, H4), 8.51 (s, 4H, H2), 7.97 (s, 2H, Bz-H3,6), 7.57 (d, 4H, J=10.5 Hz, H6), 6.93 (dd, 4H, J=9.5, 10.5 Hz, Hs), 3.95 (s, 12H, CO,Me), 3.19 (sept, 4H, J=7.0 Hz, iPr), 1.40 ppm (d, 24H, J= 7.0 Hz, iPr); 13C NMR (100 MHz, CDCl;): 8 = 165.4, 150.7, 145.2, 142.9, 141.5, 139.2, 138.2, 136.9, 134.3, 128.0, 124.9, 115.2, 108.7, 92.8, 91.0, 51.2, 39.2, 24.6 ppm; HRMS (ESI) calcd for [G4H620s+Na]*: 1101.4342; found: 1101.4337; elemental analysis calcd (%) for C4H620*⅓H,0: C 81.89, H 5.82; found: C 81.94, H 5.82.

Methyl 5-isopropyl-1-(1,1,4,4,-tetracyano-2-phenyl-3-butadienyl)azulene1-carboxylate (12): To a solution of 5 (164 mg, 0.50 mmol) in ethyl acetate (10 mL) was added TCNE (96 mg, 0.75 mmol). The resulting mixture was stirred at room temperature for 30 min under an Ar atmosphere. The solvent was removed under reduced pressure. The residue was purified by column chromatography on silica gel with ethyl acetate and BioBeads with CH,Cl, to give 12 (220 mg, 96%). Red crystals; m.p. 175.0-
